gloss.en: an area of activity, interest or knowledge

words.example: This idea belongs to the realm of science.

gloss.en: at or near the back of something

words.example: The car has front and rear windows.

gloss.en: the process of thinking in a logical way

words.example: What is the reasoning behind this decision?

gloss.en: to make somebody feel less worried or afraid

words.example: They tried to reassure her, but she still felt anxious.

gloss.en: a person who fights against their government

words.example: The rebel forces attacked the city.

gloss.en: a violent attempt to change a government

words.example: The north of the country rose in rebellion against the government.

gloss.en: a person who receives something

words.example: She was the recipient of the award.

gloss.en: the process of rebuilding or restoring something

words.example: They began the reconstruction of the educational system.

gloss.en: to tell somebody about something that happened

words.example: He began to recount the events of that night.

gloss.en: a public vote on an important issue

words.example: Switzerland decided to hold a referendum on joining the EU.

gloss.en: an image seen in a mirror or shiny surface

words.example: He admired his reflection in the mirror.

gloss.en: a change made to improve a system

words.example: The new government is committed to reform.

gloss.en: shelter or protection from danger or trouble

words.example: A further 300 people have taken refuge in the US embassy.

gloss.en: an act of saying you will not do something

words.example: His refusal of the offer surprised everyone.

gloss.en: to get back something you had lost

words.example: I struggled to regain some dignity.

gloss.en: despite difficulties; paying no attention

words.example: The weather was terrible but we carried on regardless.

gloss.en: a system of government, often an unfair one

words.example: The country was ruled by a military regime.

gloss.en: a body that officially controls an industry

words.example: The regulator fined the company for unfair practices.

gloss.en: having power to control and oversee an industry

words.example: The plan was approved by regulatory bodies.

gloss.en: helping somebody return to a normal, healthy life

words.example: He spent six months in a drug rehabilitation centre.

gloss.en: the period during which a king or queen rules

words.example: By the end of his reign, the empire was in decline.

gloss.en: the act of refusing to accept something

words.example: Her proposal met with unanimous rejection.

gloss.en: a close connection with the subject discussed

words.example: I don't see the relevance of your question.

gloss.en: the quality of being able to be trusted

words.example: The incident cast doubt on her reliability.

gloss.en: unwilling and hesitant to do something

words.example: He finally gave a reluctant smile.

gloss.en: the people, things or time that remain

words.example: I kept some of his books and gave away the remainder.

gloss.en: the parts of something that are left over

words.example: She fed the remains of her lunch to the dog.

gloss.en: a way of dealing with a difficult situation

words.example: There is no easy remedy for this problem.

gloss.en: something that makes you remember something

words.example: The size of the cathedral is a constant reminder of religion's power.

gloss.en: the act of taking something away

words.example: The work required the removal of a number of trees.

gloss.en: to cause something to be in a certain state

words.example: The accident may render him unable to work.

gloss.en: to begin something again after a pause

words.example: The army renewed its assault on the capital.

gloss.en: famous and respected

words.example: She is a renowned author.

gloss.en: money paid to use something for a time

words.example: Telephone charges include line rental.

gloss.en: the act of replacing one thing with another

words.example: The replacement of worn car parts is expensive.

gloss.en: according to what some people say

words.example: The band have reportedly decided to split up.

gloss.en: the act of presenting somebody in a certain way

words.example: The film offers a negative representation of single mothers.

gloss.en: to make a copy of a picture or text

words.example: It is illegal to reproduce these worksheets without permission.

gloss.en: the process of producing young or copies

words.example: We studied sexual reproduction in plants.

gloss.en: a country governed by elected leaders, not a king

words.example: The country became a newly independent republic.

gloss.en: to look like or be similar to something

words.example: She closely resembles her sister.

gloss.en: to live in a particular place

words.example: He returned to Britain after residing abroad for many years.

gloss.en: a house, especially a large or grand one

words.example: They live in a desirable family residence.

gloss.en: suitable for living in; made up of houses

words.example: They live in a quiet residential area.

gloss.en: a small amount left at the end of a process

words.example: There were pesticide residues in the fruit.

gloss.en: the act of giving up your job or position

words.example: He handed in his letter of resignation.

gloss.en: opposition to a plan, idea or change

words.example: As with all new ideas it met with resistance.

gloss.en: belonging separately to each one mentioned

words.example: They are specialists in their respective fields.

gloss.en: in the same order as those just mentioned

words.example: Julie and Mark are aged 17 and 19 respectively.

gloss.en: the work of repairing an old building or painting

words.example: The restoration work took several years.

gloss.en: a rule or fact that limits what people can do

words.example: The government has imposed export restraints on some products.

gloss.en: to begin again after an interruption

words.example: The two sides agreed to resume talks.

gloss.en: a movement away from danger or defeat

words.example: We studied Napoleon's retreat from Moscow.

gloss.en: to bring or get something back

words.example: She bent to retrieve her comb from the floor.

gloss.en: a surprising fact that is made known

words.example: There were sensational revelations about her private life.

gloss.en: hurting someone because they hurt you

words.example: She is seeking revenge for the murder of her husband.

gloss.en: to change something to the opposite

words.example: The court decided to reverse the earlier decision.

gloss.en: an improvement in strength or condition

words.example: We are seeing a revival of trade.

gloss.en: to become or make healthy and strong again

words.example: The flowers soon revived in water.

gloss.en: connected with political revolution

words.example: He became a revolutionary leader.

gloss.en: persuasive speech that may not be sincere

words.example: We grew tired of the rhetoric of political slogans.

gloss.en: a long gun held to the shoulder to fire

words.example: He carried a hunting rifle.

gloss.en: a violent disturbance by a crowd in public

words.example: One prison guard was killed when a riot broke out.

gloss.en: to tear something quickly or violently

words.example: I ripped my jeans on the fence.

gloss.en: a set of actions always done the same way

words.example: They performed the religious rituals.

gloss.en: strong and healthy

words.example: She was almost 90, but still very robust.

gloss.en: the hard solid material of the earth's surface

words.example: They drilled through several layers of rock to reach the oil.

gloss.en: a long straight piece of wood or metal

words.example: The concrete is reinforced with steel rods.

gloss.en: to turn around a central fixed point

words.example: Stay clear when the blades start to rotate.

gloss.en: moving in a circle around a fixed point

words.example: We studied the daily rotation of the earth on its axis.

gloss.en: an official decision made by an authority

words.example: The court will make its ruling on the case next week.

gloss.en: a story people talk about that may not be true

words.example: It is easy to start a rumour but hard to stop it.

gloss.en: to dismiss somebody from a job

words.example: She was sacked for refusing to work on Sundays.

gloss.en: connected with God; considered holy

words.example: They visited a sacred temple.

gloss.en: giving up something important for a greater aim

words.example: They made a great sacrifice to send their son to college.

gloss.en: a very holy person honoured by the Church

words.example: The church was named after a famous saint.

gloss.en: the benefit or purpose of something

words.example: She moved to the country for the sake of her health.

gloss.en: an official order limiting trade with a country

words.example: Trade sanctions were imposed against the country.

gloss.en: the right to give your opinion on a decision

words.example: We had no say in the decision to sell the company.

gloss.en: spread far apart over a wide area

words.example: There were a few scattered settlements in the valley.

gloss.en: having doubts that something is true

words.example: She looked highly sceptical.

gloss.en: the opportunity or room to do something

words.example: There's still plenty of scope for improvement.

gloss.en: a metal pin with a thread to fasten things

words.example: One of the screws is loose.

gloss.en: careful and complete examination

words.example: Her argument doesn't stand up to scrutiny.

gloss.en: an official mark stamped on a document

words.example: The letter bore the president's seal.

gloss.en: not connected with religion or spiritual matters

words.example: He prefers secular music.

gloss.en: appearing to be true but maybe not

words.example: It was a seemingly stupid question.

gloss.en: a part that is separate from the others

words.example: She cleaned a small segment of the painting.

gloss.en: to take something suddenly with force

words.example: She tried to seize the gun from him.

gloss.en: not often; rarely

words.example: He had seldom seen a child with so much talent.

gloss.en: involving only a small chosen number

words.example: They practise the selective breeding of cattle.

gloss.en: a member of a senate

words.example: The senator gave a long speech.

gloss.en: a feeling caused by something affecting your body

words.example: I felt a burning sensation on my skin.

gloss.en: the ability to understand others' feelings

words.example: She showed great sensitivity to the needs of children.

gloss.en: a feeling or opinion based on emotion

words.example: We saw the spread of nationalist sentiment.

gloss.en: the act or state of being separate

words.example: The state's separation from the federation took years.

gloss.en: doing the same thing many times in the same way

words.example: The police caught a serial killer.

gloss.en: a way of organizing something; a system

words.example: I've only been here two weeks and don't know the set-up.

gloss.en: an official agreement that ends a dispute

words.example: They worked hard to negotiate a peace settlement.

gloss.en: feelings linked to a person's sexual desires

words.example: He was confused about his sexuality.

gloss.en: an owner of shares in a company

words.example: She is one of the major shareholders in the company.

gloss.en: to break suddenly into small pieces

words.example: He dropped the vase and it shattered into pieces.

gloss.en: to get rid of something no longer wanted

words.example: The factory is shedding a large number of jobs.

gloss.en: used to emphasize size, degree or amount

words.example: The area is threatened by the sheer number of tourists.

gloss.en: ships in general or as a group

words.example: The canal is open to shipping.

gloss.en: a new part that grows on a plant

words.example: The plant has new green shoots.

gloss.en: to become or make smaller in size

words.example: My sweater shrank in the wash.

gloss.en: to raise and drop your shoulders to show doubt

words.example: Sam shrugged and said nothing.

gloss.en: a long breath showing tiredness or relief

words.example: She let out a sigh of relief.

gloss.en: to recreate real conditions for study or training

words.example: The software can simulate conditions on the seabed.

gloss.en: an artificial copy of real conditions for study

words.example: They ran a computer simulation of how the planet functions.

gloss.en: at the same time as something else

words.example: The game will be broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio.

gloss.en: an offence against God or moral law

words.example: He believed it was a sin to lie.

gloss.en: located in a particular place or position

words.example: My bedroom was situated on the top floor.

gloss.en: to draw a quick picture with few details

words.example: The artist began to sketch the old castle.

gloss.en: to move forward with light little jumps

words.example: She skipped happily along beside me.

gloss.en: to shut something loudly and with force

words.example: I heard the door slam behind him.

gloss.en: to hit somebody with the flat of your hand

words.example: She slapped his face hard.

gloss.en: to make a long cut, often violently

words.example: Someone had slashed the tyres on my car.

gloss.en: the state of being forced to work as a slave

words.example: Many people were sold into slavery.
